product.name: ArMoto
product.categorySourceGroup: Heavy Warp Knit Fabrics for Shoes and Wind Jackets
product.titleDescriptor: Fabric for Shoes and Office Upholstery
descriptions: ArMoto can be used on motorcyclist cloths as air channel.
For the shoes industry it is popular fabric to laminate on base and edge of sport shoes.
Fabric for Shoes and Office Upholstery
technicalParameters: Dimensinal Change | -5% < Length < +5% ; -5% < Width < +5% | TS EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

evidence: ArMoto can be used on motorcyclist cloths as air channel.
acceptedMatches.rationale: Product family explicitly named for motorcycle apparel and description states use on motorcyclist clothing as air channel fabric.
Explicit hi-vis property combined with EN ISO 20471 certification directly supports high-visibility application.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Mentioned use in sport shoes lamination, but no explicit athletic garment or performance-sport application stated.

evidence: ArMoto can be used on motorcyclist cloths as air channel.
primary.rationale: The product family and description explicitly identify this fabric as designed for motorcyclist clothing (wind jacket air channel), making motorcycle apparel the dominant, most direct end use.

evidence: For the shoes industry it is popular fabric to laminate on base and edge of sport shoes.
titleDescriptor: Fabric for Shoes and Office Upholstery
secondary.rationale: A separate, explicitly stated end use in shoe manufacturing (lamination on base and edge of sport shoes) independently supports footwear-components as a secondary application.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Hi-vis property and EN ISO 20471 norm are listed, but no explicit garment application text ties this fabric to certified high-visibility clothing use; property/standard alone is insufficient per rules.
Title mentions 'Office Upholstery' but no further descriptive support beyond a title fragment; overshadowed by explicit motorcycle and shoe use statements.
Mentioned only in context of sport shoe lamination, not as a standalone athletic apparel application.
